text.content:
This system implements Neon City Overdrive and the other Action Tales games by Peril Planet.

To pick your game, open Game Settings → Configure Settings → Neon City Overdrive and other Action Tales! and set the Game Line (Neon City Overdrive, Star Scoundrels, Dungeon Crawlers, Hard City, or Tomorrow City). Then click Apply Recommended Settings to switch the optional rules to that game's recommended configuration. You can also customize which rules rules are active homebrew experience.

Dice rolls are done by clicking on various Traits to add action or danger dice to the global dice pool which can then be rolled. You can shift-click a trait to invert the type of dice added. Players can also make a simple roll by clicking on the dice icon on their character sheet or using the NCO Roll macro in the compendium.

Click the lock icon at the top right of a character sheet to toggle between edit mode and play mode.

  • You can create Trademarks as reusable items to be drag and dropped onto the character sheet, or create them directly on the character sheet. You can add Triggers to Trademarks and upgrade them to Edges by clicking on the + icon next to them.

  • You can customize a characters starting Conditions in the System Settings, or edit them after a character has been created.

  • Characters support a number of optional fields like Advantages and Ties from Tomorrow City. Enable these in the System Settings.

  • Vehicles are another actor that can be used as a Character or Threat depending on token disposition and actor ownership.

GMs can create Threats, Scenes, and Jobs. Threats and Scenes can be drag-and-dropped on Jobs to organize a session.

  • Pressure (optional rule): Enable to add Pressure rule from Star Scoundrels to the game. The Pressure track is pinned near the top of the screen; each uncancelled 6 on a Danger die ticks it up by one.

  • Phase-based initiative (optional rule): Automates the initiative roll, splitting the PCs before and after threat phases. Track which characters have taken their action in the combat tracker. Enable if not using an alternative method to track initiative.

Everything configurable lives under Game Settings → Configure Settings → Neon City Overdrive and other Action Tales!

  • The Game Line setting re-themes every sheet, dialog, and chat card and swaps in that game's terminology (e.g. Stunt Points become Flow in Star Scoundrels and Moxie in Hard City / Tomorrow City). Changing it requires a reload.

  • Apply Recommended Settings switches the optional rules to the recommended configuration for the selected line in one click: the Drive and Stash tracks for Neon City Overdrive, Pressure for Star Scoundrels, Ties for Hard City, Cred/Ties/Advantages for Tomorrow City, and so on. Each rule can still be toggled individually afterwards.

  • Other settings control starting Hits and Stunt Points, the starting Conditions new characters are seeded with, the Stunt Point options listed in chat, track lengths, and how clicking a track behaves.
